MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-09-292714 Conservatorship of Catherine DeMartini.
Appearances: Herman Frank for and with Lori DeMartini and for and with Paul DeMartini. Lawrence Siracusa (Probate Counsel) and Brian McCaffrey (Litigation Counsel) for and with Elizabeth Adler. Emilie Calhoun for Thomas DeMartini. Ron Robles also present. Linda Kovacich also present.

The Conservatorship is on calendar only for the purpose of continuing Mr. Feder's fee petition to August 25, 2011. The Parties presented oral argument. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: 1) The Motion to Disqualify Counsel will remain on calendar for August 5, 2011 and is to be heard at 10:00 a.m. 2) Mr. Huster's petition for attorney fees is continued to August 5, 2011 at 10:00 a.m. at the same time as the Motion to Disqualify Counsel. Mr. Huster may advise the court if he wishes to submit his petition for fees based upon his prior argument. Mr. Siracusa is to notify Mr. Huster. 3) The Petition for Revocation, Recission, etc. and the Petition for Restitution of Trust Property, etc. were continued to August 25, 2011 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. 4) Thomas DeMartini's/Ms. Calhoun's Motion for Attorney's fees was taken under submission. 5) The Petition to Sell the house is granted. The personal property items in the house requested by Thomas DeMartini may be removed by Thomas DeMartini no later than August 29, 2011. Thomas DeMartini is to schedule an appointment with Ms. Adler for a date to pick up the items. Ms. Adler is authorized to hire Pinkerton's to supervise the pick up so it can be verified that no items other than those on his list are removed. Lori DeMartini, Paul DeMartini and Ron Robles are authorized and ordered to remove all other items of personal property between August 30, 2011 and September 30, 2011. To the extent they can agree upon which items belong to whom they may schedule a day to pick up those items. All items to which there is no agreement must be removed by Lori, Paul and Ron to a storage unit at their expense no later than September 30, 2011. If there are items of personal property remaining in the home after September 30, 2011, they will be deemed to be abandoned by Lori, Paul and Ron. Lori, Paul and Ron are to schedule no more than two moving days. Ms. Adler is authorized to hire Pinkerton's to supervise the removal of property for no more than two days. Thomas DeMartini shall have between October 1, 2011 and October 15, 2011 to claim any personal property items remaining at the house. He may schedule a time with Ms. Adler to go to the house to retrieve the items. Pinkerton's will be hired to supervise the removal of any items by Thomas. Ms. Adler is authorized to sell or dispose of any items of personal property remaining at the house after October 15, 2011. The cost of Pinkertons for Thomas will be paid by the trust to be deducted later from Thomas's share of proceeds. The cost of Pinkertons for Lori, Ron and Paul will be paid by the Trust to be deducted later and charged equally to Lori's and Paul's share. No one, other than Ms. Adler, may enter the house except on days scheduled with Ms. Adler and at which Pinkerton's is present. Mr. Siracusa is to prepare the order granting sale of the property which is to include the above.

Mr. Huster stated that he had his response and that he would file it today. Mr. Siracusa stated that it was not necessary to consolidate the cases, that Mr. Huster should give a timeline with regard to conducting discovery and that he intended to file an ex parte request to hire Daniel Conrad for Litigation Counsel. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accounting, conducting of discovery, not having received information with regard to the distributions, turning over records and expenses not being for the benefit of Catherine Demartini. The Court encouraged the Parties to open the lines of communication and cooperate with regard to identifying the expenses for Lori and Thomas. The matter was continued to June 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status of exchange of information and for setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Mr. Huster stated that he was withdrawing portions of the petition with regard to removal of the Temporary Trustee and appointment of an independent Temporary Trustee. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to payments to Ms. Wright, Ms. Adler's roll as a Trustee, the care plan, missing funds and assets, the well being of Catherine DeMartini, Ms. Paolini's reapresentation for Ms. DeMartini and the estate planning.
